blurb: Apple's secure boot chain establishes a cryptographic chain of trust from immutable Boot ROM through each subsequent boot stage. On Apple silicon Macs, the Boot ROM loads and verifies the Low-Level Bootloader (LLB), which loads iBoot; on A-series devices Boot ROM loads iBoot directly. Each stage uses Apple's public key to verify a digital signature before passing control to the next. The LocalPolicy mechanism on Mac with Apple silicon encodes the user's chosen security configuration into a cryptographically signed structure managed by the Secure Enclave, ensuring only authorized operating systems and boot configurations can run.
